title: Fill gaps and PR all Tier 3 tools
description: 9 gaps across 4 tools, then stage everything, run tests, commit, push, PR.
close_reason: Verified complete: all 6 Tier 3 tools (llm_audit, telecom_attack, evasion, phishing, grpc_audit, auth_audit) have tool class + registered parser + tests + lg_tools wiring + ATT&CK rules. Closed by commit 1ae27d5.

title: Operator Console — Round 2 (knowledge, monitoring, engagement, audit)
description: Second sprint extending the webview operator console (apps/web + operator_api/), following PRs #37-#41. Four child features, each = one operator_api endpoint + one UI surface/panel. All four touch the same files (operator_api/routes.py, server.py wiring, apps/web/src/lib/api.ts + types.ts, apps/web/src/app/App.tsx), so ship as sequential rebased PRs to avoid conflicts. Recommended order: knowledge -> audit -> engagement -> live-monitoring (most architectural last).

title: Console: Knowledge search surface
description: Add a 'Knowledge' left-rail surface that queries the hybrid store. BACKEND: wrap knowledge/store.py KnowledgeStore.hybrid_search(query, k, filter_table) -> [{table, source_id, preview, distance}]. Add GET /api/knowledge/search?q=&k=&table= in operator_api/routes.py via a new knowledge_search callback wired in server.py (store via _get_store(), guard None). FRONTEND: new Surface 'knowledge' in App.tsx, search box + optional table filter, ranked result list (source_id, table badge, preview, score). Add api.knowledgeSearch + KnowledgeHit type. Lowest risk, highest value — do first.

title: Console: Audit / activity log view
description: Surface the audit JSONL trail. BACKEND: wrap audit.py audit_logger.get_recent(n, session_id) -> records {ts, session_id, tool, args, result_summary, duration_ms, success, trace_id}. Add GET /api/audit/recent?n=&session_id= callback in server.py + route in routes.py. FRONTEND: audit view (new surface or right-panel tab), reverse-chron list, filter by success/tool, show duration + result_summary. Independent of the others.

title: Console: Engagement auto-refresh + finding detail + report
description: Deepen the existing engagement monitor. BACKEND: extend operator_api/engagement.py build_engagement_status to include findings[].detail (already on the finding record in tools/engagement.py, just not surfaced). Add GET /api/engagement/report returning generate_report() markdown / report.md path (workspace/report.md). FRONTEND: poll /api/engagement on an interval while active (stop when idle); expandable finding rows showing detail; a 'View report' action rendering/downloading the markdown. Pure additive.

title: Console: Live agent monitoring (async task registry + cancel)
description: ARCHITECTURAL — do last. Today graph/agent.py run_manual_subagent is strictly fire-and-await: no registry, scheduler is None, no progress, no cancel. WORK: (1) introduce an in-process running-agents registry (task id, type, description, status, started_at, asyncio.Task handle); (2) make /api/subagents/run + /batch launch as tracked background tasks returning a task id instead of blocking; (3) GET /api/agents/running list + POST /api/agents/{id}/cancel (task.cancel()). FRONTEND: running-agents list with status + cancel, poll while any active, surface output on completion. Largest blast radius — touches the subagent execution model; keep the synchronous path working or migrate the Subagents surface carefully.

title: BLE full scanning + config-only backfill
description: Tier 1 items 3+4: Add BLE scanning to flipper.py (ble_scan, ble_connect, ble_enumerate, ble_read, ble_write), implement 3 config-only stubs (bettercap_mitm, subghz_bruteforce, nfc_write), update enforcement/docs/tests.

title: protoAgent bring-back round 3: self-driving core + platform robustness
description: Audit of protoAgent v0.39->v0.60 (ADR 0030->0059) selected high-value autonomy + robustness pieces for protoPen's self-driving pentest-companion north star (headless-primary, tight security, cherry-pick not full-platform). Tiers: T1 self-driving core (scheduler robustness, wait/resume, background subagents, monitor cadence tick); T2 platform primitives (graph/sdk.py: Supervisor/telemetry/Knobs + mid-turn steering); T3 agent hygiene (dream/forget_memory, user_only skills, set_goal verifier safety). Explicitly SKIP: ACP runtime, plugin epic (bundles/workspaces/pluggable middleware+knowledge), console IA epic. Every shipped piece must pass scripts/smoke.sh on the Steam Deck (deck@steamdeck :7870).

title: State plumbing: wire ResearcherState into create_agent + InjectedState session reads
description: FOUNDATION (blocks wait + background subagents). graph/agent.py:568 omits state_schema, so create_agent runs on the default messages-only AgentState and a per-turn session_id never reaches tool bodies. protoAgent hit this as the root cause of 3 bugs (wait resumed in wrong thread, set_goal/background lost origin session): tracing.current_session_id() reads EMPTY inside a LangGraph tool body. Fix: pass state_schema=ResearcherState (graph/state.py already has session_id) to the lead create_agent, stamp session_id into state per turn, and read it via InjectedState in tool bodies (contextvar kept only as off-graph fallback). Acceptance: a tool can read the originating session_id reliably; regression test drives a real graph turn (not a mock). Source: protoAgent ADR 0053 fix, CHANGELOG v0.41/0.42.
close_reason: Wired state_schema=ResearcherState; session_id + context now real channels (verified in g.channels + input schema). Added session_id_from_state() helper + AuditMiddleware session attribution. Tests: tests/test_state_session_injection.py (4, incl. behavioral InjectedState read via fake model). Full suite 1497 passed. Committed on feat/state-plumbing-session.

title: QA: Steam Deck full smoke + deploy verification (round 3)
description: Gate for the whole epic. After each shipped piece: rebuild the web bundle if touched, deploy to the Deck (git pull + rebuild per the known deploy gotcha), restart the :7870 service, and run scripts/smoke.sh on the Deck (ssh deck@steamdeck 'bash -s' < scripts/smoke.sh) — all checks green. Plus feature-specific live checks: wait resume fires after N s; a backgrounded task notifies on completion; a monitor goal's cadence tick fires its hook with no turn; scheduler resumes jobs after a service restart. Record results on this issue.
close_reason: Epic QA gate satisfied. Deck-validated across all PRs: #220 core (smoke 25/25 + 6/6 live A2A model-turn tests), #222 Tier2/3 (31/31 tests + live dream), #223 follow-ups (13/13 tests + live /dream slash via A2A). Released v2.0.40 + v2.0.41. Only 1hw.11 (frontend live-render) deferred (headless-primary).

comments.author: Josh Mabry
comments.text: Deck smoke baseline (main, pre-epic): 22/25 PASS. The 3 FAILs are all the live chat turn (no COMPLETED) — environmental, NOT code: the Deck model gateway api_base=http://ava:4000/v1 returns 401 (runtime status api_key_configured:false; logs show 401 on /v1/embeddings). Live model-turn validation on the Deck (wait-resume firing a turn, background subagent completing, chat steering) is BLOCKED until ava:4000 key is fixed. Non-model paths (scheduler firing, job persistence, API surface, A2A contract, web bundle) ARE validatable on the Deck, plus full local fake-model tests.

comments.author: Josh Mabry
comments.text: Deck validation of self-driving core (F0+T11+T12+cadence) on feat/self-driving-core @3dc5268, Deck Python 3.13: (1) new tests 35/35 pass on the Deck's own venv; (2) service boots clean in 8s on the new code; (3) runtime status shows goal.monitor_interval_s=60; (4) boot log: 'monitor-goal ticker started (every 60s)' + scheduler acquired owner-lock cleanly; (5) 'wait' present in /api/tools; (6) full smoke 22/3 — IDENTICAL to baseline, no regressions (the 3 fails remain the ava:4000 gateway blocker, unrelated). Deck restored to main afterward. Live model-turn behavior (wait-resume firing a real turn) still gateway-blocked.

comments.author: Josh Mabry
comments.text: GATEWAY FIXED (operator updated ava key in Infisical). Full live Deck validation of the self-driving core on feat/self-driving-core @db47438: smoke 25/25 (the 3 chat-turn checks now PASS — gateway works, no regressions). Live A2A model-turn tests 6/6: (background ADR 0050) turn completes + job started + completion notification token echoed on next turn; (wait/resume ADR 0053) turn yields + scheduler resumes in-thread + token recalled. Monitor cadence ticker confirmed running at boot (unit-tested; live-achieve not exercised — verifier-semantics dependent). Deck restored to main after. Tier-1 core Deck-validation COMPLETE; this gate stays open for Tier 2/3.

comments.author: Josh Mabry
comments.text: Tier 2/3 Deck validation (feat/platform-primitives-r3, Py 3.13): 31/31 new tests pass on the Deck venv; service boots clean; smoke 25/25 (no regressions, gateway live). Live dream subagent run via /api/subagents/run on the real gateway succeeded — it called memory_list, reviewed 50 facts, and produced a consolidation report flagging real duplicate facts by #id (Steam Deck/Portainer/LAN-host counts). sdk primitives / steering / user_only / goal-safety are unit-tested + boot-clean on the Deck. Deck restored to main. PR #222 (all gates green) pending merge.

title: Frontend: live-render scheduler/wait-resumed turns (chat.resumed)
description: Follow-up to T12 (wait/yield/resume, protopen-1hw.3). When the scheduler fires a wait-resume (or any scheduled turn) into a chat session the browser is viewing, the turn runs server-side and persists to history but the open ChatSurface doesn't render it until a refetch. Port protoAgent's pattern: emit a chat.resumed event on the bus when a scheduler-fired turn lands in a chat context, and a ChatResumeWatch that appends the resumed answer live (display-only; backend owns history). Low priority — headless-primary; deferred from T12.

comments.author: Josh Mabry
comments.text: DEFERRED (round-3 judgment, 'as needed'): protoPen is headless-primary (UI verification low-priority per project notes). This is a React console component (ChatResumeWatch) + a backend chat.resumed event; the backend event has no standalone consumer without the frontend, and the frontend live-render delivers little for a headless deployment. Leaving filed for when the Deck display-friendly UI work happens. The resumed turn already persists to history and is retrievable; only live in-browser rendering is missing.

title: Cancellable single delegation (cancel one in-flight task, keep the turn)
description: Follow-up to T22 (mid-turn steering, protopen-1hw.6). Port protoAgent's Tier-2 control: a per-session registry of in-flight task() delegations (asyncio.Task wrappers) + POST /api/delegations/{tool_call_id}/cancel to abort ONE delegation without killing the parent turn (user-cancel swallows CancelledError → graceful 'cancelled' string; parent-turn cancel re-raises). graph/delegations.py + wire into _build_task_tool. Deferred from T22 to keep that PR focused on steering.
close_reason: graph/delegations.py registry + task tool runs foreground delegations as a registered cancellable asyncio.Task (keyed by injected tool_call_id); operator cancel -> graceful string, parent-turn cancel re-raised. GET /api/delegations + POST /api/delegations/{id}/cancel. Tests: tests/test_delegations.py (3). Full suite 1567. feat/round3-followups.

title: On-demand /skill <name> runner (invoke a skill, incl. user_only)
description: Follow-up to T32 (user_only skills, protopen-1hw.8). protoPen skills are retrieval-only; add server-side slash dispatch so a skill can be RUN on demand: /skill <name> looks up the skill (incl. user_only, via SkillsIndex.get_skill) and injects its prompt_template as the turn instruction. Must work on the PRIMARY A2A streaming path (_chat_langgraph_stream currently handles only /goal — general slash dispatch lives in the non-stream chat()). Makes user_only skills (excluded from auto-retrieval) actually invocable. Add to _CHAT_COMMANDS + /api/chat/commands.
close_reason: Progressive disclosure: <available_skills> catalog (name+desc) + load_skill(name) tool (always-on); user_only catalog-hidden but loadable. /skill <name> runs a body on demand on both chat paths. Config skills.progressive_disclosure (default on; legacy path kept+tested). Tests: test_progressive_skills.py (5), test_slash_skill_dream.py (7). Full suite 1564. feat/round3-followups.

title: /dream slash + scheduled cadence default for the dream subagent
description: Follow-up to T31 (protopen-1hw.7). Convenience: add a /dream slash command (runs the dream subagent via run_manual_subagent, like /purple) and optionally seed a default scheduled cadence (e.g. weekly) so memory consolidation runs unattended without the operator hand-scheduling it. Dream is already delegable via task() + /api/subagents/run; this is ergonomics.
close_reason: /dream slash runs the dream subagent (both chat paths); goals.dream_cadence_cron seeds an idempotent recurring /dream job at startup. Tests in test_slash_skill_dream.py. feat/round3-followups.

title: KnowledgeBackend protocol — thin pluggable-backend seam (ADR 0031)
description: Adopt only the ADR 0031 interface (no registry/config selector, no alt backends): a runtime_checkable KnowledgeBackend Protocol capturing the swappable retrieval + semantic-fact + stats surface (search/keyword_search/hybrid_search/add_fact/list_facts/delete_fact/get_stats). KnowledgeStore is the default impl; security-domain methods stay off the contract. Future-proofs a pgvector/Qdrant swap. From the original round-3 audit shortlist.
close_reason: knowledge/backend.py runtime_checkable KnowledgeBackend Protocol (search/keyword_search/hybrid_search/add_fact/list_facts/delete_fact/get_stats); KnowledgeStore is the default impl (docstring note); conformance test locks names+signatures + asserts security-domain methods stay off the contract. Interface-only (no registry/selector/alt backends). Tests: tests/test_knowledge_backend.py (3). Full suite 1570. feat/knowledge-backend-protocol.

title: Scheduler: owner-lock retry + startup catch-up resilience
description: protoPen scheduler/local.py has NO owner-lock or startup catch-up. On Deck restart/redeploy the prior process can hold the port while draining a turn; protoAgent's bug was the scheduler logging 'owned by another live instance' and NEVER starting, so every scheduled job + resume silently stopped firing until an unrelated reload. Port: (1) retry the jobs-db owner-lock in the background (~15s) and start polling the moment it frees; (2) on startup catch-up, a POST to /a2a refused before uvicorn is accepting connections logs a concise INFO 'agent not reachable yet; will retry' instead of an ERROR traceback. Acceptance: kill+restart the service on the Deck and confirm scheduled jobs resume firing within seconds (no manual reload). Source: protoAgent CHANGELOG v0.41.
close_reason: flock owner-lock with background retry (never blocks startup) + release on stop; _fire ConnectError/ConnectTimeout -> concise INFO not ERROR traceback. Tests: 4 new (contention, release, self-heal, concise connect-error), 20/20 scheduler suite. Committed feat/self-driving-core.

title: wait / yield / resume tool (ADR 0053)
description: Absent in protoPen. Add a wait(seconds, then) tool: the turn ends immediately and the scheduler re-invokes the agent N seconds later IN THE SAME chat session with history intact + the 'then' instruction. Eliminates busy-wait polling during long scans/exploits (core long-horizon primitive). Port: tools/lg_tools.py wait tool reading session from InjectedState (stamp job context_id = originating session); a WaitYieldMiddleware.before_model that jumps to end when a successful wait result is the trailing tool message; scheduler fires resume into job.context_id (else Activity thread). Schedule failure returns an Error string and does NOT yield. Live-render the resumed turn via an events-bus 'chat.resumed' signal. Acceptance: wait in a Deck chat resumes in that chat after N s; smoke.sh green. Source: ADR 0053, graph/middleware/wait_yield.py.
close_reason: wait(seconds,then) tool + WaitYieldMiddleware (jump-to-end on trailing wait marker) + scheduler context_id routing (resume into same a2a:{session} thread, lazy migration). Reads session via InjectedState (F0). Tests: tests/test_wait_yield.py (8) + 4 scheduler context_id/migration; full suite 1513 passed. Frontend live-render split to follow-up protopen-1hw.11. Committed feat/self-driving-core.

title: Background subagents + reactive notification (ADR 0050 Phase 1; Phase 2 via event bus)
description: Absent (current background is console fire-and-forget, not agent-loop). Add task(run_in_background=True): spawn a detached subagent as a durable self-POSTed A2A turn (contextId=background:<id>), return immediately, and drain a <task-notification> into the originating chat's NEXT turn (exactly-once via a 'notified' flag). Phase 1: background/store.py (sqlite registry) + background/manager.py spawn + an a2a terminal hook that marks complete and publishes on the event bus; turn-input assembly prepends the notification. Phase 2 (autonomous wake): protoAgent uses an inbox 'now' item — protoPen DROPPED the inbox for security, so ADAPT the wake to the existing event bus + Activity thread instead (gated, off by default). Acceptance: agent backgrounds a recon scan on the Deck, continues, and is notified on completion without polling. Source: ADR 0050.
close_reason: graph/background.py BackgroundManager (spawn detached + drain-once session-scoped + render <task-notification> + event-bus announce); task(run_in_background) reads origin session via InjectedState; chat stream prepends notifications on next turn; bus wired at startup. In-process adaptation (not durable self-POST/sqlite) — noted. Tests: tests/test_background_subagents.py (6); full suite 1523 passed. Committed feat/self-driving-core.

title: graph/sdk.py host-free primitives: Supervisor + DecisionLog/telemetry/render_html + Knobs
description: Establish a graph/sdk.py with the pure-stdlib robustness primitives protoPen lacks (all 5/5 portability). (1) Supervisor/supervise() — watchdog-backed background task lifecycle: re-kick on crash, restart on stall (frozen progress + stall_check), on_crash recovery, status() dict; pairs with T13 to keep long engines alive unattended. (2) DecisionLog (capped ring) + telemetry() envelope + render_html() panel — reusable provenance for the companion presence/steer-approve story; pure html.escape. (3) Knobs + presets + make_knob_tools — bounded, reversible, logged tuning surface (e.g. scan-aggressiveness presets) the LLM can steer. Vendor verbatim where pure; unit-test directly (host-free). SKIP the plugin testkit until/unless protoPen grows a real plugin contract. Acceptance: each primitive unit-tested in CI; Supervisor demo survives an injected crash. Source: protoAgent graph/{supervisor,telemetry,knobs}.py (#1025/1027/1028).
close_reason: graph/sdk.py re-exports pure-stdlib primitives: Supervisor/supervise (watchdog re-kick/restart/recover + status), DecisionLog+telemetry+render_html (provenance panel), Knobs+presets+make_knob_tools (bounded reversible tuning). Host-free; testkit skipped (no plugin contract yet). Tests: tests/test_sdk_primitives.py (10); full suite 1533. Committed feat/platform-primitives-r3.

title: Mid-turn steering + cancellable delegation
description: The 'steer' half of protoPen's north-star steer/approve loop. While a turn streams, POST /api/chat/sessions/{id}/steer queues a message that a SteeringMiddleware.before_model drains and folds in as a framed HumanMessage at the next model call (no stream teardown); DELETE cancels a still-pending steer; anything queued past the last model call is reconciled as a fresh turn at turn-end. Also a per-session delegations registry so the console can cancel ONE in-flight task delegation without killing the whole turn. Port: graph/steering.py, graph/middleware/steering.py, graph/delegations.py, operator_api/chat.py endpoints. Depends on F0 (session from injected state). Acceptance: steer a running Deck turn and see it folded in next model call. Source: protoAgent CHANGELOG v0.43, graph/steering.py.
close_reason: Mid-turn steering shipped: graph/steering.py queue + SteeringMiddleware (drains via InjectedState, folds framed HumanMessage) + POST/DELETE /api/chat/sessions/{id}/steer. Tests: tests/test_steering.py (6); full suite 1539. Cancellable single-delegation split to follow-up protopen-1hw.12.

title: dream: memory consolidation subagent + forget_memory tool
description: protoPen semantic facts (ADR 0021) have no supersession/TTL; a long-running companion accumulating facts across engagements needs hygiene. Port the dream HALF of ADR 0054 (skip distill skill-mining — low value offensively): a scheduled, tightly-scoped subagent (NO shell, NO raw SQL) that folds durable verified facts into long-term memory and PRUNES stale/superseded/duplicate ones by targeted id. New tools: forget_memory(chunk_id, reason) (one-id delete, no wildcard), recent_activity (read-only digest), and memory_list rows lead with #<id> for targeting. Runs on demand (/dream) or via the existing scheduler. Acceptance: /dream on the Deck prunes a duplicate fact and is reflected in the knowledge store; subagent toolset resolves (see F0/out-of-graph toolset note). Source: ADR 0054.
close_reason: dream subagent (SUBAGENT_REGISTRY) + KnowledgeStore.delete_fact + memory_list/forget_memory/recent_activity tools (scoped, no shell/SQL). Prunes stale/dupe facts one id at a time. Delegable via task()/subagents API. Tests: tests/test_dream_memory.py (5); full suite 1554. /dream slash + cadence default split to follow-up protopen-1hw.14.

title: user_only skills (slash-only, never auto-retrieved)
description: Small flag in the skills schema: mark a SKILL.md user_only so it is ONLY a /<slash> command and is NEVER auto-retrieved into context by KnowledgeMiddleware — for deliberate, run-on-demand (e.g. destructive) procedures you don't want surfaced automatically. Port: graph/skills/index.py SkillRecord + frontmatter parse; KnowledgeMiddleware retrieval filter excludes user_only; slash dispatch still resolves them. Acceptance: a user_only skill never appears in <learned_skills> but runs via its slash command. Source: protoAgent CHANGELOG v0.53.
close_reason: user_only flag shipped: SKILL.md frontmatter parsed, stored (UNINDEXED col + migration), excluded from load_skills auto-retrieval, fetchable via get_skill(name), surfaced in all_skills. Tests: tests/test_user_only_skills.py (6); full suite 1549. On-demand /skill runner (skills are retrieval-only on the A2A path) split to follow-up protopen-1hw.13.

title: set_goal verifier-type safety (ADR 0028)
description: Security hardening: confirm/enforce that the agent-callable set_goal can ONLY create goals with safe verifier types (plugin/llm/findings/etc.) and CANNOT set shell-backed (command/test/ci) or eval-based (data+eval) verifiers — those stay operator-only to prevent RCE/over-scoped execution from the model. Also validate the verifier 'check' against the registered verifiers up front so an unknown check can't create an unsatisfiable goal that spins to the iteration cap. Audit graph/goals/{controller,verifiers}.py + the set_goal tool path. Acceptance: a set_goal attempt with a shell/eval verifier from the model is rejected with a clear error listing allowed verifiers. Source: ADR 0028, CHANGELOG v0.41.
close_reason: Audit: protoPen already no-code-exec (VERIFIERS = findings/targets/task/llm only; no shell/eval). Hardened: AGENT_SAFE_VERIFIERS single source of truth + import-time asserts (subset of registry, no exec types); set_goal validates against it (was a drifting hardcoded tuple). Tests: tests/test_goal_verifier_safety.py (4). Committed feat/platform-primitives-r3.

title: Container/K8s audit tool
description: New tools/container_audit.py wrapping kube-hunter, deepce, CDK, kube-bench. Includes parser, lg_tools registration, and tests.

title: Companion: Capabilities catalog (Flipper-style B-subtext)
description: The capability-first 'B subtext' from the companion vision (autonomous-first A is the spine; this is the opt-in manual layer). A friendly, browseable catalog of what protoPen can DO (WiFi recon, OSINT, scanning, RF/hardware) — pick a capability and fire it manually, or hand it to the agent. Replaces the dense tool-list mental model. Backed by the tools/skills registry. See docs/plans/2026-06-05-companion-ui-vision.md. Depends on / fits with the IA restructure. Scope fresh.
notes: Building in Slice 4 (companion IA restructure). New GET /api/tools (operator_api/capabilities.py) categorizes the live tool registry; CapabilitiesSurface.tsx renders a searchable, category-grouped catalog with 'Ask agent' handoff (chat-store pendingDraft → Home composer). Capabilities rail tabs: Catalog · Skills · Workflows · Subagents.

title: Tier 3 roadmap implementation
description: 6 items: llm_audit, telecom_attack, evasion, phishing, grpc_audit, auth_audit. Each needs tool class, parser, tests.

title: Goals: out-of-band cadence tick for monitor goals (ADR 0030 D2.1)
description: Follow-up to the monitor-goal core (mode=monitor shipped). Add a scheduler-driven periodic evaluate of idle monitor goals so a long-horizon objective progresses with NO turn in its session (fully-background autonomy). Wire a cadence tick (runtime/state + agent_init + server) that calls GoalController.evaluate per active monitor goal; on achieved, surface via the Activity thread / terminal hook. Also consider controller.evaluate_now(session_id) for event-driven checks (ADR 0030 D2.2/PR3).
close_reason: MonitorGoalTicker (graph/goals/ticker.py): asyncio cadence loop (default 60s, config goals.monitor_interval_s) evaluates active monitor goals out-of-band with no turn; announces achieved via event bus (goal.achieved) + Activity thread. Wired in server/app.py startup/shutdown; STATE.monitor_ticker; surfaced in runtime status. Tests: tests/test_monitor_ticker.py (4); full suite 1517 passed. Committed feat/self-driving-core.

title: Terminal: reconnect + scrollback persistence
description: Keep the PTY session alive on WS disconnect and replay scrollback on reconnect (protomaker's session registry + scrollbackBuffer in apps/server/src/services/terminal-service.ts). Lets a terminal survive a browser reload, not just in-app nav. Backend (server/terminal.py) session registry keyed by id + a ring buffer; frontend reconnects with the session id.

title: Terminal: recursive split panes (Alt+D/Alt+S + spatial nav)
description: Follow-up to the terminal MVP (v2.0.33). Add the recursive split-pane tree from protomaker: {type:'terminal'|'split', direction:'horizontal'|'vertical', panels[]} per tab, Alt+D/Alt+S to split, Ctrl+Alt+arrows for spatial pane navigation, drag-to-resize. Frontend-only on the existing /ws/terminal backend. protomaker refs: apps/ui/src/components/views/terminal-view.tsx + store/terminal-store.ts. See project_integrated_terminal memory.

title: Terminal: theme picker + font zoom
description: Port protomaker's terminal theming (Dracula/Nord/Tokyo Night/etc.) and per-pane font zoom (Cmd/Ctrl+scroll, +/- controls) to the integrated terminal. Default stays the Pilot Protocol skin.

title: Brainstorm: Container/K8s audit + WebSocket testing tools
description: Design two new tool modules for protoPen: container_audit.py (kube-hunter, deepce, CDK, kube-bench) and WebSocket testing (extend web_vuln.py or new tool). Follow existing BasePentestTool patterns.
close_reason: Completed — container_audit + websocket_test shipped in commits 2bd06ca and 0a10cbd

title: Remove nanobot legacy backend
description: Remove all nanobot backend code, config, and references. LangGraph is the sole agent runtime going forward.

title: Write spec: container_audit + websocket_test tools
description: Write design doc covering both new tools, parser registration, lg_tools wiring, and test plan.

title: Port protoAgent tool-call cards to the console chat (#282 + #288)
description:
Render the agent's tool activity as live collapsible cards in the React operator console chat — the last of four protoAgent ports (the other three landed & deployed: code-split #69, compaction #70, slash-autocomplete #71). Ports protoAgent PRs #282 + #288.

CURRENT STATE (backend ~half-there):
- server.py::_chat_langgraph_stream yields ("tool_start", ...) / ("tool_end", ...) but as FLATTENED emoji strings ("🔧 name: preview"), not structured data.
- a2a_handler.py (~line 402) already handles tool_start/tool_end event types.
- Console renders assistant text + markdown but shows NO tool cards.

SCOPE (port from local protoAgent clone /Users/kj/dev/protoAgent):
Backend
- server.py: emit STRUCTURED frames ("tool_start", {id,name,input}) / ("tool_end", {id,output}) keyed by langchain run_id so start/end pair up.
- a2a_handler.py: carry the structured tool_event through the A2A SSE DataPart (TaskRecord.last_tool_event + update_state(tool_event=...)); keep text status for back-compat.
Frontend (apps/web/src/)
- lib/types.ts: ToolCall + wire shape (id,name,input,output,status running|done|error,durationMs,parentId).
- lib/api.ts: parse tool-event DataParts in the SSE stream into message.toolCalls[].
- chat/ToolCalls.tsx + chat/tool-renderers.tsx: port (generic structured renderer; per-tool icons; running→done/error pill; durations; copy-to-clipboard; nest subagent task children under parent).
- chat/ChatSurface.tsx: render <ToolCalls calls={message.toolCalls} /> in assistant messages.
- app/theme.css: tool-card styles — ADAPT protoAgent's violet vars to protoPen's green terminal theme (--accent #3ee07a, --bg-raised, --border-strong), same as the slash-menu in #71.
Tests
- tests/test_a2a_tool_events.py (mirror protoAgent).

VERIFY: live in-browser (screenshot) — drive a chat turn on the Deck, watch cards stream tool_start→running→done. Then deploy (git pull + npm run web:build + systemctl --user restart protopen.service) + hard-refresh.

NOT applicable / confirmed clean: protoAgent #302/#304 (output truncation / scratch_pad leak) and #300 (token streaming) are tied to graph/output_format.py, which protoPen does NOT have.
close_reason: Code shipped: PR #72 (d13e27d) merged tool-call cards; the A2A 1.0 migration then broke console chat, now fixed by PR #148 (port web client to 1.0) — verified live on the Deck that the agent streams task->6x tool-call-v1 DataParts->artifact->COMPLETED, and the deployed bundle uses native 1.0. In-browser screenshot gate waived: operator runs protoPen headless-primary, and a Steam Deck display-friendly UI rework is planned that will revisit these cards.

title: mush.bike Active Engagement — Phase 2
description: Run 7 recommended active-mode scans against mush.bike
close_reason: mush.bike engagement was a separate session — not current work

comments.author: Josh Mabry
comments.text: :443 TLS audit timed out (testssl >5min). :8443 timed out at 180s — port likely closed/firewalled/not TLS. Need to retry :443 with shorter scope or individual checks.

comments.author: Josh Mabry
comments.text: TLS 1.2+1.3 only (excellent). ECDHE-ECDSA ciphers, QUIC/H3 offered. Dual RSA+ECDSA certs from Google Trust Services, valid until 2026-07-11. OCSP stapling, CT logs present. HSTS MISSING (MEDIUM), no DNS CAA record (LOW). Post-quantum MLKEM hybrid key exchange on TLS 1.3. Port 8443 not responding.

comments.author: Josh Mabry
comments.text: No Swagger/OpenAPI docs exposed on mush.bike or api.mush.bike. All common API paths (/api, /v1, /graphql) return 404 on both hosts. API likely uses non-standard paths or is behind WAF.

comments.author: Josh Mabry
comments.text: Subdomains found: api.mush.bike, www.mush.bike. www.mush.bike -> mush-web.onrender.com -> Cloudflare edge (216.24.57.7, 216.24.57.251). Amass not installed, subfinder only.

comments.author: Josh Mabry
comments.text: CORS: clean on both :443 and :8443. Security headers: ALL 5 MISSING on :443 (X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, HSTS, CSP, X-XSS-Protection). Port 8443 returns 403 (Cloudflare WAF block). MEDIUM finding logged.

title: WAF Bypass Test — direct origin access

comments.author: Josh Mabry
comments.text: WAF bypass FAILED — both IPs (216.24.57.7, 216.24.57.251) are Cloudflare anycast in SEA. Port 80 → 301 redirect. Port 8080 → 403 WAF block. Port 8443 → dead. CF 403 page leaked "onrender.com" backend platform. True origin not reachable via direct IP.

title: Companion IA: restructure rails to companion nouns
description: Restructure the console IA from the operator-console rails (Chat/Intel/Agents/System) to the autonomous-first companion nouns: Home/Companion · Engagement · Findings · Activity · Capabilities · System. Target shape + rationale in docs/plans/2026-06-05-companion-ui-vision.md (the candidate table) and the upstream-IA tightening notes (flatten to top-level rails, one shallow sub-nav each). Re-homes the misfiled 'skills' tab and collapses the overloaded 'Intel' rail. Control stack (goals/workflows/playbooks) becomes the autonomy engine behind Engagement, not a rail. Companion presence (shipped #184) is the seed of Home. Larger effort — scope into slices before building.
notes: Slices 1+2 SHIPPED + released in v2.0.29 (2026-06-06). S1 rails regroup (PR #187), S2 Home/Companion presence surface (PR #189, HomeSurface.tsx — presence hero + engagement summary + needs-you HITL router + findings ticker; shared useCompanionState). REMAINING: S3 Engagement-as-object, S4 Capabilities catalog (= protopen-1vd). Plan: docs/plans/2026-06-06-companion-ia-restructure-slices.md
